# ALFRED recorded fixtures (v1.3 item I)

The cassettes in this directory are vcrpy YAML recordings of a small
panel of ALFRED/FRED real-time API calls (`UNRATE`, `CPIAUCSL`,
`FEDFUNDS`; 5 vintages per series). They are committed verbatim so
`tests/test_alfred_real_recorded.py` can exercise the PIT ingestion path
in CI without network access.

## Re-recording procedure

Set a real FRED API key, set `MRE_ALFRED_RECORD=1`, and run the recorded
test once:

```bash
export FRED_API_KEY=<your key>
export MRE_ALFRED_RECORD=1
pytest tests/test_alfred_real_recorded.py -q
unset MRE_ALFRED_RECORD
```

The test will rewrite the cassettes against the live FRED endpoint.
Commit the changes; CI will then play them back without a key.

## Synthetic vs. live cassettes

The cassettes shipped with v1.3 are **synthetic** — generated by
`tests/fixtures/alfred/conftest_helpers.py` from a deterministic seed,
not from a real FRED call. The data shape, key names, and date ordering
match the v2 ALFRED contract exactly, so the lineage invariants the
test asserts (observation_date ordering per vintage,
`realtime_start == vintage_date` per row, no null `value`s) are
exercised against bytes that look like real FRED bytes.

When you re-record (above), the synthetic cassettes are replaced with
the live recording. The test does not care which produced the bytes —
only that the playback yields rows that pass the invariants.

This split exists because:

1. We cannot ship live FRED data into CI (no API key, schema can drift).
2. We do not want the regression test to disappear when the cassettes
   are missing (which is what would happen with a `pytest.skipif`).

The synthetic cassettes therefore cover the schema contract; the
re-record loop covers the live-data contract whenever someone with a
key wants to validate it.
